## s4_2_identity_building_closure · section heading
> I feel like all of our Identity Building stuff sucks. We probably need writers to hand write some of this for a bit and then maybe train up the AI on finished product. Right now all of this makes me want to barf
**Issue:** The entire identity building closure section feels generic, hollow, and lacks authentic voice
**Step:** `section_structurer`  [high]
**Reasoning:** This is a transition section with dialogue beats that were initially generated by section_structurer. The reviewer's complaint is about the overall quality and authenticity of identity building content, not specific voice violations that dialogue_rewriter would catch. The dialogue reads like templated motivational content ('real mathematical thinking', 'you proved it', 'get ready') rather than genuine reflection. Section_structurer is responsible for the initial content and structure of these beats.
**Fix:** Hand-write identity building closures to establish authentic voice patterns, then use those as training examples for section_structurer
**Prompt file:** `steps/prompts/section_structurer.py`

## s4_2_identity_building_closure · beats[1] (dialogue)
> I hate this less than the rest of Identity Building but I also don’t really understand what it means by “Bags”
> This is a good summary of what they did in the 2nd half of the unit. Giving the through-line of the learning from the introduction to multiplication to the commutative property. (Bags and circles are how they represented groups of items - bags with actual concrete items like oranges and then circles with dots as more abstract)
**Issue:** The dialogue references 'bags and circles' without explaining what they are, causing confusion for reviewers unfamiliar with earlier unit content.
**Step:** `section_structurer`  [high]
**Reasoning:** The section_structurer generated this closure dialogue and chose to reference 'bags and circles' as representations of equal groups. While the second comment clarifies this was pedagogically appropriate (bags/circles were used earlier in the unit), the first reviewer's confusion indicates the reference lacks sufficient context. The structurer is responsible for the initial content and wording of dialogue beats, including deciding what prior concepts to reference and how explicitly to explain them.
**Fix:** Add brief clarifying context when referencing 'bags and circles' (e.g., 'You found equal groups—bags of oranges, circles with dots') or provide the explanation in the scene beat's montage description.
**Prompt file:** `steps/prompts/section_structurer.py`
**Input prompt for this section:** `tracked_scripts\u1\m12\synthesis\step_05_section_structurer\prompts\s4_2_identity_building_closure.md`

## s4_2_identity_building_closure · beats[5] (dialogue)
> This seems out of place… get ready for what? It’s the end of the unit… what are they preparing for? haha. What’s a better closing statement? @Dayne McClurg
> Agree. See my above comments. Are we even including this “Identity Building” stuff in current ed-tech? I assume all Identity Building that we are generating is going to be trash. We probably need to improve the language in the starter packs (or whatever generative doc is structuring this)
> This is pulling from the SP. I don’t know if we killed this part in Unit 5. I don’t know if it is also in future units or if it got killed before other units SPs were produced? I get why you don’t like the identity builder, mathematical thinking, like a mathematician…. but some of it I do personally like because I think there is value in building the identity of kids to connect with being a “real mathematician”. But definitely not a hill I will die on!
**Issue:** Closing dialogue says 'Get ready' at the end of the unit, which is contextually inappropriate and lacks meaningful closure.
**Step:** `section_structurer`  [high]
**Reasoning:** This is a top-level dialogue beat in a transition section. The section_structurer is responsible for the initial dialogue wording and content. The beat appears unchanged through the pipeline (same text in steps 5, 6, 9, 11, 12), meaning dialogue_rewriter did not flag it as a voice/style violation. The issue is content appropriateness — the dialogue doesn't make sense as a closing statement — which is section_structurer's responsibility.
**Fix:** Replace with contextually appropriate closing dialogue that reflects completion rather than anticipation, or remove the beat entirely if no further content follows.
**Prompt file:** `steps/prompts/section_structurer.py`
**Input prompt for this section:** `tracked_scripts\u1\m12\synthesis\step_05_section_structurer\prompts\s4_2_identity_building_closure.md`
